SimulationParameters.label: Simulator Stats
SimulationParameters.tooltip: Original empirical statistics (Win Rate, Averages...) captured during the initial simulation workflow.

max_drawdown.label: Maximum Drawdown
max_drawdown.tooltip: Don't panic. This is your historical maximum pain. It shows the level of drawdown the strategy routinely survives without breaking (the cost of doing business).
max_drawdown.chartGuide: This curve is your 'chill pill'. If it shows, for example, P74, it means 74% of the strategy's entire operational time is actively spent enduring this exact amount of pain or less. It's its natural habitat, let it work.

ev.label: Expectancy
ev.tooltip: Mathematical Expectancy is analytically governed by the formula (Win Rate*W) - (1-Win Rate)*L. Conceptually: multiply your expected wins by your average profit, and subtract the damage of your expected losses by your average loss. It strictly defines the expected net profit per trade (the foundational pillar of the Bayesian engine).
ev.chartGuide: Distribution of the expected value per trade. Measures the magnitude of your statistical Expectancy.

survivalProbability.label: Survival Probability
survivalProbability.tooltip: Mathematical probability that the system will continue to make money in the long term after calculating variance.
survivalProbability.chartGuide: Your Expectancy converted into a survival percentage against ruin. Less than 50% is flipping a coin.

equitySimulation.label: Monte Carlo
equitySimulation.tooltip: Projection of 100 possible paths your equity could take based on your Expectancy.
equitySimulation.chartGuide: Visualize the cone of uncertainty. Some paths sink fast due to bad luck (variance), others skyrocket. The goal is for dispersion to remain stable upwards.

daily_loss.label: Daily Loss Limit
daily_loss.tooltip: The maximum loss suffered during a single trading day.
daily_loss.chartGuide: Theoretical probability of reaching certain daily loss ranges. Vital for adjusting Daily Drawdown limits for Prop Firm evaluation tests.

stagnation_days.label: Stagnation Days
stagnation_days.tooltip: The longest period (in days) the strategy has taken to recover to a new all-time high in profit.
stagnation_days.chartGuide: The distribution predicts how many days equity usually remains stagnant before recovering. Fat tails imply months in temporal Drawdown.

stagnation_trades.label: Stagnation Trades
stagnation_trades.tooltip: Maximum number of trades executed during a Drawdown phase before reaching a new historical peak.
stagnation_trades.chartGuide: Reflects how many statistical 'shots' it costs on average to get out of a hole. Helps distinguish if a strategy is broken or just needs more trades to recover.

consecutive_losses.label: Consecutive Losses
consecutive_losses.tooltip: The maximum number of back-to-back losing trades.
consecutive_losses.chartGuide: Risk of losing streaks. The model estimates the probability of suffering N losses in a row, which is crucial to avoid blowing up the account.

live_max_drawdown.label: Live Drawdown
live_max_drawdown.tooltip: The negative floating your account assumes right now. Look at its percentile (e.g. P74): it means that 74% of the time, the strategy peacefully coexists with drawdowns of this size or smaller.

live_daily_loss.label: Live Daily Loss
live_daily_loss.tooltip: Your accumulated loss for today's calendar day (closed + floating). The counter resets exactly at midnight (UTC).

live_stagnation_days.label: Live Stagnation Days
live_stagnation_days.tooltip: The consecutive days you have been stuck in a relative drawdown since the last all-time high.

live_stagnation_trades.label: Live Stagnation Trades
live_stagnation_trades.tooltip: The amount of live trades executed sequentially without having achieved a new profit peak yet.

live_consecutive_losses.label: Live Consecutive Losses
live_consecutive_losses.tooltip: The ongoing streak of losing trades accumulated up to this exact moment.

guardian_status.label: Guardian Status
guardian_status.tooltip: Statistical test panel (P-Values) for live operations Win Rate, Streak and PnL. Detects immediately if there's any statistically significant deviation compared to the Backtest (green/yellow/red LEDs).
guardian_status.chartGuide: Consolidated status of live consistency tests.

master_verdict.label: Master Verdict
master_verdict.tooltip: The final verdict calculated by the Bayesian Engine that summarizes if the system still holds its statistical edge or should be halted as a precaution.

bayes_blind_risk.label: Blind Risk
bayes_blind_risk.tooltip: Probability that your statistical edge does not exist. 1 - P(Expectancy > 0). The higher this number, the more likely you are trading without a real advantage.

bayes_degradation.label: Delta Expectancy
bayes_degradation.tooltip: Measures the impact of real market friction (slippage, spread, fills) compared against your simulations. Formula: ((Adjusted Expectancy - Backtest Expectancy) / |Backtest Expectancy|) * 100. \nExample: If your Backtest projected $10 of profit per trade and your Adjusted Expectancy dropped to $5 in Live, the Delta will be -50% (Degraded).

bayes_weight.label: Empirical Weight
bayes_weight.tooltip: Current credibility ratio. Indicates whether the statistical engine is trusting your actual Live trades or the original Backtest history more to calculate your probabilities. Note: The Backtest is capped at a maximum weight of 30 trades. This ensures that Live performance gains relevance rapidly, acting as an early protection mechanism before a broken strategy reaches historical drawdowns, and establishing a fair asymmetric standard to compare the degradation across all strategies.

live_trades_count.label: Live Trades
live_trades_count.tooltip: Total number of real trades executed in Live for this strategy. More Live trades increase the Bayesian engine's confidence in the adjusted metrics. 🟢 100+ trades = high confidence, 🔵 30+ = significant data, ⚪ <30 = early stage where backtest still dominates.

bayes_p_positive.label: P(Expectancy>0)
bayes_p_positive.tooltip: Bayesian probability that the strategy has a positive expected value (edge). If this value is high (e.g. 85%), it means there is an 85% probability that the strategy generates money per trade over time. It is the complement of Blind Risk: P(EV>0) = 1 - Blind Risk.

connection.api_tooltip: Server Status. Indicates if the web dashboard can reach the IronRisk API in real-time.
connection.ea_tooltip: Expert Advisor (EA) Heartbeat. Shows how long ago MetaTrader 5 successfully sent the live risk telemetry you are seeing.

workspaceSettings.title: Workspace Settings
workspaceSettings.subtitle: Global configuration for the {account} account. Applies to all EAs you use.
workspaceSettings.masterRiskDesc: Enabling a limit here forces all EAs in this account to evaluate that risk. Disabling it shuts off risk scrutiny ACROSS the whole account (overriding any individual strategy settings).

thankYou.title: You're on the list!
thankYou.desc: Thank you for joining the IronRisk waitlist. We'll notify you via email as soon as Closed Beta spots become available.

workspaceManager.pageDesc: Each trading account acts as an isolated workspace with its own API token and strategies.
workspaceManager.title: Trading Accounts
workspaceManager.description: Create a virtual container for each of your MetaTrader terminals (e.g., "FTMO 100k"). Each account gets its own unique connection API token.
workspaceManager.confirmDeleteDesc: This action is completely IRREVERSIBLE. Are you sure you want to delete this account AND all its strategies?
workspaceManager.emptyState: No active trading accounts. Create one to get started.
workspaceManager.guidedDesc: To start processing Bayesian data in real-time, create your first Workspace and link it to a MetaTrader terminal.
workspaceManager.guidedStep2Desc: Required to encrypt the connection and prevent data leaks between accounts.
workspaceManager.guidedBindWarning: 💡 If you leave the account number empty, IronRisk will auto-detect it when the Service connects for the first time from your MetaTrader. The token will be permanently bound to that account.
workspaceManager.tutorialStep1: Click ⚡ Auto-Installer (above). The .bat file will be downloaded completely pre-configured.
workspaceManager.tutorialStep2: Double-click the .bat file. Pick your terminal from the command prompt and answer 'y' to restart. Zero configuration.
workspaceManager.tutorialStep3:
Start the MetaTrader Service

1. In the left panel of MT5 (Navigator), expand the Services folder until you reach its last child (IronRisk_Service).
2. Right-click on it and select Start.

With this, it will stay connected permanently and you will be able to see it running in green both on the web and normally in your terminal.
workspaceManager.lockedTooltip: Install and connect the EA bot on your MetaTrader terminal first. The workspace will unlock once we receive the first heartbeat.
workspaceManager.tooltipStartDate: Boundary date: trades collected in your MT5 account after this date are considered Live
workspaceManager.resetDefault: Reset to default (1 day before first trade)
workspaceManager.waitingLive: No live trades yet — waiting for EA sync
workspaceManager.clickMetricDist: Click a metric to see its distribution
workspaceManager.optionUploadDesc: Upload the file with your equity curve: Strategy Tester CSV, or HTML/XLS reports exported from MetaTrader.
workspaceManager.optionManualDesc: Declare your expected parameters or start from scratch
workspaceManager.optionManualBlocked: A manual strategy (magic=0) already exists in this workspace
workspaceManager.batchDesc: Import multiple files at once with auto-detection
workspaceManager.phaseWaitingDesc: No live data yet. Projecting from backtest only.
workspaceManager.phaseCalibratingDesc: Calibrating — {n} live trades. Not enough statistical power yet.

landing.betaNoCodeMsg: Don't have a code? IronRisk is in Closed Beta by invitation. Ask @IronRisk_Ivan on Telegram.
landing.heroBridge: Upload your backtest. Connect your account. Get a verdict in real time.
landing.heroDesc: Your backtest has already answered that question thousands of times. IronRisk connects your statistical history to your live account in real time — so you know whether what you're seeing is a real warning sign or just expected noise. Before you touch anything.
landing.heroTag: Your backtest, alive and watching your account 24/7. Zero assumptions, pure statistical context.
landing.heroScreenshotCaption: Real IronRisk dashboard — Live Risk Verdict
landing.caseContext: The backtest shows this happens 94% of the time.
landing.diagTitle: Your system has survived this before. Your backtest proves it.
landing.diagDesc: When the market goes against you, your backtest knows if this is normal variance. But it's buried in a file you're not looking at. You're making decisions with a red number on screen and zero context.
landing.diagLead: Your backtest has the answer. The problem is it's not available when you need it most.
landing.diagPunch: IronRisk puts it in front of you, in real time, every time you doubt.
landing.step1Desc: Upload your Strategy Tester report — CSV, HTML or Excel. Or define your metrics manually in the simulator. We automatically verify which probability distribution fits your data.
landing.step2Desc: Max Drawdown, daily loss. The thresholds your rational self sets when the market is closed.
landing.step3Desc: Download our auto-installer. It places the firewalls in your MetaTrader and establishes secure telemetry autonomously. You'll be protected in seconds.
landing.cureDesc: Set your limits when you're rational, with the market closed. IronRisk applies them when you're staring at a red number with no context. That way the decision is made with your own data, not your gut.
landing.without2: You close the losing trade prematurely
landing.without4: You destroy your system's statistical edge
landing.with2: You see your backtest had drawdowns of -$2,400
landing.with3: Your brain receives the signal: "my data says this is expected"
landing.with4: You let the system work without intervention
landing.notKillDesc: IronRisk does not lock your screen or force-close your trades. We hate losing control of the account just as much as you do. We give you the statistical context you need to make that call yourself, with data, not with your gut.
landing.ctaTitle: The right question already has an answer.
landing.ctaDesc: Your backtest already knows whether what you're seeing live is normal or a real signal. IronRisk shows you before you make any decision.
landing.ctaJoin: Analyze your strategy for free. Upload your backtest and discover in seconds if your system has a real statistical edge.
landing.ctaIndie: IronRisk is an 'indie' tool, built by an independent algo-trader. Pure craftsmanship, no rush, no corporate pressure.
landing.ctaPrivacy: 🛠️ Indie tool. No investors. No pressure to monetize your data.
landing.feat1Desc: Get a push notification on your phone when a risk metric crosses your limit. You don't need to be in front of MT5 — the system watches your back 24/7.
landing.feat2Desc: Click any risk metric and see your current position plotted on the probability bell curve from your backtest. Your drawdown stops being a number and becomes a percentile you can interpret.
landing.feat3Desc: Running strategies on a VPS? If your system disconnects — because the VPS shut down, MT5 crashed, or the connection dropped — IronRisk detects it instantly and alerts you via Telegram. Stop finding out your strategy has been offline for hours.
landing.showcase1Desc: The Monitor analyzes your live metrics against your backtest and issues an instant verdict: CONSISTENT, DEVIATION, or ANOMALY. Includes your Blind Risk — the Bayesian probability that you're trading without a real edge. No subjective interpretation, pure statistics.
landing.showcase2Desc: See your expectancy bell curve and your real equity side by side. If the green zone dominates, your trades confirm an edge. If the red grows, your results look more and more like noise. No opinions — just what your own numbers say.
landing.showcase3Desc: All alerts from all your strategies, centralized. Every time a metric enters unusual territory, you get a notification with the exact statistical context.
landing.showcase4Desc: Click any metric and visualize exactly where you stand on the probability bell curve. Your drawdown stops being a number and becomes a percentile you can interpret.
landing.showcase5Desc: Five risk metrics, five progress bars, five percentiles. At a glance you know if your drawdown, daily loss, stagnation days, and losing streaks are within expected range. No charts needed.

themeSelector.descWorkspace: Select a custom flavor just for this workspace.
themeSelector.descInline: Assign a specific visual style to this account.
themeSelector.forceAll: Force all workspaces to use this theme

themeBuilder.title: Theme Configurator
themeBuilder.subtitle: Design your own custom IronRisk flavor

simulate.title: Analyze Your Strategy
simulate.subtitle: Discover the statistical survival probability of your equity curve.
simulate.guideManual: If you don't have a file with your strategy's trade history, but you know its average performance, enter your manual metrics below. We will calculate your Survival Probability.
simulate.guideCsv: Upload your MetaTrader trade history. We will automatically extract your statistical Expectancy and project your real equity curve.
simulate.winRateTooltip: Strict percentage of trades that end in profit.
simulate.avgWinTooltip: The exact average profit per winning trade.
simulate.avgLossTooltip: The exact average loss per losing trade.
simulate.stdWinTooltip: Measures how much your wins vary from the average. Example: if your avg win is $60 and std dev is $20, your winning trades typically range between $40 and $80. 💡 If you don't know this value, leave the default (~50% of your average win is a reasonable estimate).
simulate.stdLossTooltip: Measures how much your losses vary from the average. Example: if your avg loss is $60 and std dev is $20, your losses typically range between -$40 and -$80. Excessive deviation = inconsistent Stop Loss. 💡 If you don't know this value, leave the default (~50% of your average loss).
simulate.nTradesTooltip: Time projection of the simulation. For example, 200 trades might represent 1 year of trading.
simulate.dragDrop: Drag and drop your MT4/MT5 History file here, or click to select
simulate.gaugeSubtitle: Verdict from IronRisk's Bayesian engine based on your statistics.
simulate.gaugeDisclaimerDesc: Backtest data inherently suffers from over-optimization. IronRisk applies an institutional discount to this static projection to evaluate your edge with maximum rigor. The live market will be the ultimate judge.
simulate.gaugeMessageGreen: Historical data shows a real edge. Now the question is: when this strategy goes against you in live trading, will you know if it's normal variance or a real warning signal? That's exactly what IronRisk solves.
simulate.gaugeMessageOrange: You are statistically on a tightrope. A losing streak of normal variance could wipe your account before your weak Edge acts in your favor. It requires iron discipline.
simulate.gaugeMessageRed: ⚠️ Math Warning: The probabilities of sustaining positive expectancy are worse than flipping a coin. Trading this is mathematically gambling in a casino.
simulate.blindRiskLow: In this fraction of simulated universes, your strategy destroys you if you trade without defined limits.
simulate.blindRiskMedium: Too many scenarios end in red. Without a firewall, a normal variance streak can wipe your account.
simulate.blindRiskCritical: More than half of simulated scenarios end in total loss. Trading this without protection is mathematical Russian roulette.
simulate.ctaLock: Your strategy survives in {pPositive} of scenarios
simulate.ctaDesc: But without live monitoring, you won't know if you're in the {blindRisk} that destroys you — until it's too late.
simulate.errorFields: Please fill in all required fields.

inlineOnboarding.title: Early access to IronRisk.
inlineOnboarding.desc: Join the waitlist. We will reach out as soon as a spot opens.
inlineOnboarding.loginDesc: Log in to link this analysis to your existing account.

simulate.forkTitle: How do you want to start?
simulate.forkFileDesc: Upload the file with your equity curve (Strategy Tester CSV · MetaTrader HTML/XLS reports)
simulate.forkManualDesc: Enter your trading parameters and we'll simulate your risk profile
simulate.realAccountQ: Does this history come from a real account?
simulate.realAccountYes: Yes, these are real executed trades
simulate.realAccountNo: No, it's from a backtest or optimization tool
simulate.realAccountNote: Real data doesn't need statistical correction
simulate.backtestNote: A correction for potential over-optimization will be applied
simulate.casinoGateTitle: Your system has no statistical edge
simulate.casinoGateDesc: With a survival probability below 50%, trading this system is statistically equivalent to gambling. IronRisk won't help you lose money faster.
simulate.casinoGateAdvice: Review your system, adjust your parameters and come back when the numbers speak in your favor.
simulate.enteringEmail: We sent you a welcome email — check your spam/junk folder if you don't see it!
simulate.requiredFields: All required fields (*) must be filled.
simulate.betaCodeHint: IronRisk is in private beta. Enter the code you received.
simulate.magicHint: Leave 0 if you don't use EAs or don't know your magic number.
simulate.bindWarning: ⚠ Your token will be strictly bound to this account number to protect the validity of your risk data.

wizard.title: Strategy Wizard
wizard.noAccounts: No trading accounts. Please create one in Trading Accounts first.
wizard.descriptionPlaceholder: Brief description of your strategy logic
wizard.multipleStrategiesDesc: Import several CSVs at once with auto-detected names and magic numbers.
wizard.deleteAllConfirm: ⚠️ Delete ALL strategies? This cannot be undone.
wizard.step2Desc: Upload your Strategy Tester CSV. We'll detect the columns and let you map them.
wizard.dropCsv: 📄 Drop your CSV here or click to browse
wizard.dropCsvFormats: Supports any CSV format (MT4/MT5, custom, etc.)
wizard.step3Desc: Set the hard stops that the system will monitor in real-time.

paywall.upgradeDesc: This feature is only available in a higher tier.

admin.adminWarning: Warning: Actions performed here execute raw SQLAlchemy cascade deletions. Proceed with extreme caution. Deleted workspaces and strategies cannot be recovered.

glossary.w_label: Avg Win (average gain per winning trade)
glossary.l_label: Avg Loss (average loss per losing trade)

step1.title: Step 1 — Win Rate
step1.modelDesc: Conjugate Beta-Bernoulli. Each trade is a win or loss. The Beta is the natural prior — no goodness-of-fit test required.
step1.confBt: Backtest Confidence: {conf}% → each Backtest trade equals {eff} live trades

step2.title: Step 2 — W (Average Win per Trade)
step2.modelDesc: Conjugate Normal-Inverse-Gamma → posterior t-Student. The t-Student is NOT assumed — it is a mathematical consequence of the NIG. The assumption is that the distribution of the MEAN is Normal, guaranteed by the Central Limit Theorem (CLT) for n > 30.
step2.calcMeanDesc: The posterior mean is a weighted average between the Backtest (discounted) and the live data:
step2.varianceDesc1: The variance of the mean is calculated as:
step2.varianceDisp: ← dispersion of the {count} winning Backtest trades (ALL are used, not discounted)
step2.varianceNote: 💡 s² measures how much the trades vary from each other (Backtest measures this well), but n_eff reflects how much we trust the Backtest mean ({n_eff} vs {n_bt} real).
step2.actuarialP1: Assuming a t-Student for a strictly positive metric like PnL is conceptually imperfect, given its (-∞, +∞) support. However, we maintain this parametric model for two fundamental reasons:
step2.actuarialP2: 1. Degrees of Freedom & CLT: Even if individual trades are not Normal, the Central Limit Theorem ensures their sample mean converges toward a Normal distribution. The t-Student absorbs variance uncertainty with df = n_eff - 1. With an adequate sample size, negative 'heavy tails' become statistically negligible.
step2.actuarialP3: 2. Analytical Delta Method: Using 0-bounded distributions (e.g., Gamma or Weibull) would break our ability to linearly propagate analytical uncertainty for Expectancy. The NIG model provides 'asymptotically robust statistics' that maximizes clarity and interpretation.
step2.actuarialQuote: «All models are wrong, but some are useful.» — George E.P. Box

step3.title: Step 3 — L (Average Loss per Trade)
step3.modelDesc: Same NIG model as step 2. Losses are treated as absolute values (positive) for easier interpretation.
step3.varianceDisp: ← dispersion of the {count} losing Backtest trades (ALL are used, not discounted)

step4.title: Paso 4 — Expectancy (Método Delta)
step4.modelDesc: Analytical uncertainty propagation. Assumes independence between Win Rate, W, and L.
step4.uncertaintyDesc: σ is calculated by propagating the uncertainty of each variable (Win Rate, W, L) using a first-order Taylor approximation:
step4.fromStep2Desc: scale² of the posterior t-Student for the average of wins
step4.fromStep3Desc: scale² of the posterior t-Student for the average of losses

step5.title: Paso 5 — P(Expectancy > 0)
step5.modelDesc: Comes from the same Normal distribution as Step 4 → never contradicts the CI.
step5.phiDesc1: Φ is the cumulative distribution function (CDF) of the Normal distribution.
step5.phiDesc2: Φ(x) = "what percentage of the bell curve area falls to the LEFT of x?"
step5.sbsDesc: From step 4 we know that Expectancy is distributed as a Normal with:
step5.question: Question:
step5.questionText: "How much of the bell curve area is above $0?"
step5.calc1: 1. We normalize 0 to units of σ (z-score):
step5.calc1Result: This means $0 lies {val}σ to the {dir} of the mean.
step5.calc2Result: ← {pct}% of the area is to the left of 0 (loss zone)
step5.calc3: 3. We invert to get the positive area:
step5.finalResult: There is a {pct}% probability that the strategy has a positive edge.
step5.cltTitle: Why do we assume a Normal distribution (Central Limit Theorem)?
step5.cltDesc1: Even though your individual trades DO NOT follow a Normal distribution (they usually have 'fat tails', asymmetric distributions, etc.), the Central Limit Theorem guarantees that the MEAN of those trades does converge to a Gaussian bell.
step5.cltDesc2: Since Expectancy is simply the product of sample means (Win Rate, Winning PnL and Losing PnL), the joint distribution of the expected value becomes strictly Normal by asymptotic convergence. That's why this calculation isn't a 'rough estimate', it's the mathematical reality of your long-term risk.

ui.title: Bayesian Engine
ui.subtitle: Statistical Inference of Survival Probability
ui.gaugeTooltipLim: Percentage consumed of your Physical Limit (Ulysses Pact)
ui.guardianDesc: Are your live results consistent with what the Backtest promised?

emptyState.desc: Waiting for live EA trades to start monitoring.

winRate.title: 1. Win Rate — Binomial Test
winRate.q: Question: "If the Backtest says Win Rate = p, what is the probability of seeing ≤ {k} wins in {n} trades?"
winRate.red: If p < 2% → 🔴 live Win Rate is abnormally low compared to Backtest.

streak.title: 2. Loss Streak — Geometric Probability
streak.q: Question: "If the Backtest says I win {pct} of the time, is it normal to see this many consecutive losses?"
streak.logic1: If your real Win Rate was the Backtest's Win Rate, each trade has a {pct} chance of being a loss.
streak.logic2: For <k>k</k> consecutive losses to occur, that probability is multiplied <k>k</k> times:
streak.red: If p < 2% → 🔴 extremely rare streak, Backtest likely misrepresents reality.
streak.yellow: If p < 10% → 🟡 unusual streak, monitor closely.
streak.green: If p > 10% → 🟢 streak within expected variance.
streak.label: Streak

pnl.title: 3. Average PnL — Normal z-test
pnl.q: Question: "Is the live average PnL consistent with the Backtest average PnL?"
pnl.compare: Compares the live mean against the Backtest mean, adjusted for sample size.
pnl.zscore: A highly negative z-score → evidence that live performance is worse than Backtest.
pnl.label: Avg PnL

avgPnl.title: 3. Average PnL — Normal z-test
avgPnl.q: Question: "Is the live average PnL consistent with the Backtest average PnL?"
avgPnl.compare: Compares the live mean against the Backtest mean, adjusted for sample size.
avgPnl.zscore: A highly negative z-score → evidence that live performance is worse than Backtest.
avgPnl.label: Avg PnL

interpretation.title: p-value Interpretation:
interpretation.desc: It's the probability of seeing results this bad or worse if the Backtest were true. A p = 2% means there is only a 2% chance these results would happen if the strategy truly works like the Backtest.

charts.evDesc: Range where the expected value (Expectancy) per trade is located with a {pct}% probability. If the interval includes 0, we cannot confirm the strategy is profitable.
charts.liveDesc: Empirical behavior. If the curve drags downward, it will pull the Gaussian bell into negative territory with each heartbeat.

ulyssesMoment.title: You're deciding cold right now.
ulyssesMoment.heroSubBt: This is what your system has already lived through. Are you ready to handle it live?
ulyssesMoment.heroSubMc: This is what the engine projects for your system. Can you take it when it happens?
ulyssesMoment.descBt: Your backtest just revealed the worst scenario your system has lived through. What you're about to do is what Ulysses did before sailing past the sirens: define your limits BEFORE the market puts you under pressure. These numbers will be your mast.
ulyssesMoment.descMc: Based on the parameters you entered, the engine has simulated thousands of possible scenarios via Monte Carlo. These are the extremes your system could go through. What you're about to do is what Ulysses did before sailing past the sirens: define your limits BEFORE the market puts you under pressure.
ulyssesMoment.ddQuestionBt: Your worst Drawdown was {value} — if you can tolerate more, raise the wall. If you want to cut it sooner, lower it.
ulyssesMoment.ddQuestionMc: It's proposed that your strategy has a max Drawdown of {value} — adjust it below to match what you're truly willing to endure.
ulyssesMoment.dailyQuestionBt: Your worst day was {value} — if you can handle that without touching anything, leave it. If not, lower the limit.
ulyssesMoment.dailyQuestionMc: It's proposed that your strategy has a daily loss of up to {value} — if that's too much, lower it. If you can take more, raise it.
ulyssesMoment.consecQuestionBt: {value} losses in a row — if your psychology can handle that streak without intervening, keep it. If not, cut it down.
ulyssesMoment.consecQuestionMc: It's proposed that your strategy has streaks of up to {value} losses in a row — adjust it to what your psychology can truly handle.
ulyssesMoment.stagDaysQuestionBt: {value} days stagnant — if you can wait that long without panicking, perfect. If not, adjust it.
ulyssesMoment.stagDaysQuestionMc: It's proposed that your strategy has up to {value} days of stagnation — if that feels like too much, cut it down. If you can wait longer, leave it or raise it.
ulyssesMoment.stagTradesQuestionBt: {value} trades without a new high — patience or panic? If unsure, lower the wall.
ulyssesMoment.stagTradesQuestionMc: It's proposed that your strategy has up to {value} trades without a new high — adjust it to what your patience can truly handle.
ulyssesMoment.coldMoment: This is your moment of clarity. Use it well.

pactoUlisesModal.title: ⚖️ ULYSSES PACT
pactoUlisesModal.tooltip:
The Ulysses Pact is your personal firewall against tilt. Here you define the maximum pain you're willing to tolerate — based on what your backtest already lived through. The system works with dual protection:

1️⃣ Bayes watches automatically — if a metric enters unusual territory (🟡 P≥85) or anomaly (🔴 P≥95), it alerts you based on your historical distribution.

2️⃣ Your wall is absolute — if the real value crosses the limit YOU set, 🚫 PACT BROKEN is declared, regardless of what the statistics say.

Configure it cold. When the market squeezes, you'll be glad you tied yourself to the mast.
pactoUlisesModal.tooltipIntro: The Ulysses Pact is your personal firewall against tilt. You define the maximum pain you tolerate with a cool head, backed by your own statistics. IronRisk works in defensive phases:
pactoUlisesModal.tooltipOutro: Build your walls now while calm. When the market squeezes, you'll be glad you tied yourself to the mast.

education.max_drawdown: The deepest valley from a peak. Sets your absolute pain threshold before considering the strategy broken or declaring technical ruin.
education.daily_loss: The daily limit stops 'revenge trading'. It protects your capital from being wiped out by a single emotional day by putting a cap on the daily loss.
education.consecutive_losses: Losing streaks are mathematically inevitable. Set your limit at the MAXIMUM NUMBER of consecutive losses you can tolerate before your psychology breaks.
education.stagnation_days: Set the NUMBER OF DAYS in a row you are willing to wait underwater without hitting a new historical high.
education.stagnation_trades: Same as days, but measured in operational wear. Set the MAXIMUM NUMBER OF TRADES in a row without returning to profit.
education.bayes_blind_risk: Bayesian blind risk limit. If the probability that your edge does not exist exceeds this threshold, you will receive an alert. The higher the Blind Risk, the more likely you are trading without a real advantage.

verdict.fatal_human: You have exceeded the pain limit you defined with a cool head.
verdict.red_human: Right now, your strategy is deviating significantly from what was expected.
verdict.amber_human: Something is starting to drift — could be normal variation, but it's no longer trivial.
verdict.green_human: Everything matches expectations. Your strategy is behaving like its backtest.
verdict.fatal_guidance: You crossed your own wall. The decision you made with a cool head applies now.
verdict.red_guidance: This is statistically rare. If it escalates, the data stops backing your strategy. If it corrects, it was normal variation.
verdict.amber_guidance: This could be normal variation. The data will give you more clarity with each trade.
verdict.green_guidance: Your strategy is operating within its known territory.
verdict.ulyssesBreachDesc: You have exceeded the strict pain threshold that you set with a cool head. Although the mathematics of your strategy may still be within normal parameters, continuing to trade exposes you to emotional decisions or revenge trading.
verdict.bayesNoAlerts: Bayes P(Expectancy>0)={pct}%. Combined Backtest+Live model estimates positive edge with no risk inconsistencies.
verdict.blindRiskLow: Blind Risk at {pct}% — edge consistent. Combined Backtest+Live model estimates positive advantage.
verdict.blindRiskSignal: Blind Risk at {pct}% — there is a significant probability your statistical edge may not be real.

humanizer.max_drawdown_green: Your drawdown is within expected range.
humanizer.max_drawdown_amber: You are approaching an unusual capital decline within your history.
humanizer.max_drawdown_red: Your current drawdown is more extreme than {pct}% of what your strategy experienced.
humanizer.max_drawdown_fatal: Your drawdown has exceeded the limit you defined.
humanizer.daily_loss_green: Today's loss is within normal range.
humanizer.daily_loss_amber: Today's loss is starting to be notable.
humanizer.daily_loss_red: Today's loss is worse than {pct}% of your historical days.
humanizer.daily_loss_fatal: Your daily loss has exceeded your limit.
humanizer.stagnation_days_green: Days without a new high are within normal range.
humanizer.stagnation_days_amber: You've been going for too many days without beating your last high.
humanizer.stagnation_days_red: You've gone more days without a new high than in {pct}% of your history.
humanizer.stagnation_days_fatal: Days without a new high have exceeded your limit.
humanizer.stagnation_trades_green: Trades without a new high are within expected range.
humanizer.stagnation_trades_amber: The number of consecutive trades without progress is getting notable.
humanizer.stagnation_trades_red: You've had more consecutive trades without a new high than in {pct}% of your history.
humanizer.stagnation_trades_fatal: Trades without a new high have exceeded your limit.
humanizer.consecutive_losses_green: Losing streaks are within expected range.
humanizer.consecutive_losses_amber: The current losing streak is getting unusual.
humanizer.consecutive_losses_red: You are on a losing streak longer than {pct}% of your history.
humanizer.consecutive_losses_fatal: The losing streak has exceeded your limit.
humanizer.blindRisk_low: The risk of trading without a real edge is low ({pct}%).
humanizer.blindRisk_moderate: There is a significant probability ({pct}%) that your edge may not be real.
humanizer.blindRisk_critical: The signals suggest your edge could be illusory ({pct}%).

riskReport.consistencyWarning: Your live {label} ({observed}) deviates from backtest expectations ({expected}). Only a {pval}% chance this is normal.
riskReport.consistencyNotable: Your live {label} ({observed}) differs from backtest ({expected}), but still within plausible range (p = {pval}%).
riskReport.bayesBlindRiskHigh: Blind Risk at {pct}% — high probability your statistical edge does not exist.
riskReport.bayesBlindRiskModerate: Blind Risk at {pct}% — moderate probability your edge may not be real.
riskReport.ddWarning: Current drawdown is at percentile {pct} of the Backtest historical distribution.
riskReport.conflictDetail: Bayesian model detects divergent signals: Blind Risk at {pct}%, but {nRed} of {nTests} consistency tests indicate live results do not match the Backtest. With few trades ({nLive}), this divergence is informative, not definitive.

alertsCenter.title: Alerts Center
alertsCenter.subtitle: Unified Management • All Strategies
alertsCenter.connectEaPrompt: Connect the EA to your account to enable Telegram
alertsCenter.step1: Open the IronRisk bot on Telegram
alertsCenter.cooldownTooltip: If set to 0, it will fire the alert only once. If greater than 0, it repeats every N minutes while you remain at risk.
alertsCenter.deleteAllConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete ALL active rules across all strategies and portfolios? This action cannot be undone.
alertsCenter.scanQrDetail: Open Telegram on your phone, scan the QR, and tap Start.

vsMode.portfolioNotSupported: VS Mode is not available for portfolios
vsMode.portfolioNotSupportedDesc: Select an individual strategy to compare with its twin in another workspace
vsMode.linkDesc: Select the same strategy in another workspace to compare execution, slippage, and divergences across environments
vsMode.matchWindowDesc: Maximum tolerance between open_time of both trades to consider them the same. For automated strategies, 60s is enough.
vsMode.noOtherWorkspacesDesc: Create another workspace with the same strategy to compare them
vsMode.unlinkConfirm: Are you sure you want to unlink this twin strategy?
vsMode.tipLinkWizard: Link here the SAME strategy running in another workspace (different account, broker, or server). The goal is to detect execution differences across environments, NOT to compare different strategies.
vsMode.tipComparison: This view compares the same strategy running in two different environments (e.g., incubator vs live, broker A vs broker B). It detects execution differences like slippage, delays, and missed trades so you can identify which environment executes better.
vsMode.tipDateFilter: Filter the comparison from the first trade date of each workspace. Useful when one account started later and you only want to see the period where both coexisted.
vsMode.tipMetrics: Global metrics for each environment. If both are the same strategy, these should be very similar. Differences reveal the real impact of the broker, server, or network conditions on your strategy's performance.
vsMode.tipDivergence: Divergence statistics between environments. Match rate shows what percentage of trades were successfully paired. Slippage shows entry price differences between environments. Hidden cost is how much money you lose (or gain) by being in one environment vs another.
vsMode.tipMatchedTrades: Trades that the matching engine identified as the same trade in both environments (same symbol, same direction, within the time window). The Δ columns show the difference between environments: ΔEntry = entry price difference, ΔP&L = profit difference.
vsMode.tipOrphanTrades: Trades that exist in one environment but were NOT found in the other. This means the strategy executed a trade in one account but not the other. This can happen due to disconnections, server time differences, or broker filters.
vsMode.suggestionDesc: We found strategies with the same name in other workspaces. Want to link them?
vsMode.tipSuggestion: When a strategy has exactly the same name in another workspace, it's very likely the same strategy running in a different environment. Link to compare performance.
vsMode.bulkMatchDesc: Automatically link all strategies that share the same name across different workspaces.
vsMode.bulkNoMatchesDesc: No strategies with the same name were found across different workspaces.

equity_curve_nan_fix.title: Equity Chart NaN Fix
equity_curve_nan_fix.body: Fixed a bug causing NaN SVG rendering errors in the equity chart when using the non-breaking space thousand separator. Fixed by explicitly setting Y-axis width.

monitor_view.title: Monitor View: Live Risk Snapshot at a Glance
monitor_view.body: New Monitor tab consolidating real-time risk status: master verdict with health traffic light, live equity chart, 5 risk gauges with educational narrative, and direct one-click access to the Bayesian Engine.

drawdown_live_chart.title: Live Drawdown Chart (Monetary)
drawdown_live_chart.body: New underwater drawdown chart synchronized below the live equity chart: shows real-time drawdown in monetary units ($), not percentages, with a synchronized cursor across both charts and hover tooltip.

gauge_names_unified.title: Unified Risk Terminology
gauge_names_unified.body: The names and educational tooltips of the 5 risk gauges are now identical across Inspector, Monitor and Bayesian Engine, localized in the user's language. Tooltips in Monitor and Bayesian Engine now use live current values, not the historical worst.

live_tab_rename.title: Tab 'Live EA' renamed to 'Live'
live_tab_rename.body: The live table view is now simply called 'Live' for greater clarity and conciseness.

risk_gauges_tests.title: Tests: Risk Gauges (DD/Stagnation/Streaks)
risk_gauges_tests.body: Added 15 regression tests for the gauge engine: green/amber/red status via percentile thresholds, fatal status when physical limit is breached (Ulysses Pact), percentile monotonicity, no-distribution fallback, and simulated flag propagation.

test_fixture_path_fix.title: Fix: Tests Self-Contained on VPS
test_fixture_path_fix.body: Regression tests were failing on the production server (0ms = immediate crash) because the demo CSV was resolved via a repo-relative path that does not exist on the VPS. Fix: CSV moved to backend/tests/fixtures/ so tests are fully self-contained and portable.

selection_mismatch_test.title: Test: Selection Consistency
selection_mismatch_test.body: Detected and documented a user-reported bug: the highlighted row in the strategy table did not match the strategy displayed in the Bayesian Engine panel. Root cause: the store auto-selects by backend array index, but the table re-sorts visually. Added 'selection_consistency' group with 5 regression tests.

regression_suite_launch.title: Regression Test Suite
regression_suite_launch.body: Launched an 18-test automated regression suite covering CSV import, Bayesian engine, and live trade updates. Results are visible directly in the Admin Panel. GitHub Actions now blocks any deploy if a test fails.

strategy_upload_fix.title: Strategy Upload Fix
strategy_upload_fix.body: Fixed a native Workspace bug where CSV uploads were losing their Magic Number and defaulting to 'Simulated Edge'. Deep MetaTrader CSV validation is now properly restored as the primary configuration path!

ea_config_hotfix.title: Dashboard Config Hotfix
ea_config_hotfix.body: The Dashboard EA now automatically reads the fresh API Token from the config file, resolving the 401 Error that occurred when reinstalling over old charts preserving old templates.

ea_autobind_hotfix.title: EA Autobind Fix
ea_autobind_hotfix.body: Fixed a bug where auto-generated workspaces prevented the MetaTrader EA from properly connecting on first use.

auth_centralization.title: Auth Centralization
auth_centralization.body: Centralized all authentication and waitlist logics in a unified modal.

waitlist_admin_mailto.title: Waitlist Mailto
waitlist_admin_mailto.body: Added mailto capability to leads table for simplified code delivery.

bayes_math_ui_cleanup.title: Universal Bayesian Math Engine
bayes_math_ui_cleanup.body: Rebuilt the mathematical breakdown into a single unified graphical component that is shared 1:1 between the Machine Learning Risk Dashboard and the Data Sandbox.

blind_risk_centralization.title: Absolute Blind Risk Centralization
blind_risk_centralization.body: The IronRisk backend Bayesian Engine is now the single source of truth for all percentages, styles, and thresholds across the entire system.

fastapi_array_hydration_fix.title: React/FastAPI Fix
fastapi_array_hydration_fix.body: Fixed React crash on FastAPI array validation.

alert_testing_fixes.title: New Testing Alerts
alert_testing_fixes.body: Added risk state transition alert simulation. Also fixed hardcoded translations in notifications.

telegram_i18n_hostname.title: Bilingual Telegram + Visible Nodes
telegram_i18n_hostname.body: The Telegram bot now responds in your language (ES/EN) and shows the server name (hostname) of each workspace so you can identify at a glance where it's connected from.

uninstaller_tool.title: Official Uninstaller
uninstaller_tool.body: Implemented a secure Auto-Uninstaller that cleans up all .ex5 files, configuration records, and IronRisk-linked entries from your MetaTrader in a single click.

dual_build_labels.title: Dual Build Labels
dual_build_labels.body: You can now instantly distinguish between the Backend (Hetzner) and Frontend (Vercel) deployment timestamps directly in the interface.

zero_touch_silent.title: Fully Silent Zero-Touch
zero_touch_silent.body: Removed the MT5 parameter dialog popup when attaching the bot. The Auto-Installer now drives the Dashboard in a completely seamless, transparent way.

user_profile_avatar.title: User Profile in Navbar
user_profile_avatar.body: Your avatar with initials now groups Theme, Language, Settings, and Sign out into a single dropdown. Fewer buttons, same functionality.

sqx_tools_center.title: Tools Center
sqx_tools_center.body: Downloads (Dashboard EA, Uploader, MagicAssigner), token copy, and tools — all accessible from your profile avatar in one click.

vps_hostname.title: VPS/Computer Visibility
vps_hostname.body: Each workspace now shows the hostname of the computer or VPS running your MetaTrader. Auto-detected during installation — no DLLs or manual setup required.

zero_friction.title: Zero-Friction Onboarding
zero_friction.body: Install the Service and Dashboard directly from the Auto-Installer with zero manual configuration. The workspace is seamlessly encrypted.

humanizer_v1.title: Humanized Bayesian Engine
humanizer_v1.body: The engine verdict now speaks in plain language. Blind Risk as the main metric, human phrases on every risk gauge, and prioritized alert signals.

email_verification.title: Email Verification
email_verification.body: You can now verify your email to secure your account. You'll receive a verification email when you register.

waitlist_magic_link.title: Waitlist with magic link access
waitlist_magic_link.body: Registration no longer requires a VIP code. Traders join the waitlist and the admin approves access with one click: a magic link is generated and emailed in the user language.

locale_switcher_fix.title: Fix locale switcher
locale_switcher_fix.body: The EN/ES switcher on register and login pages now works correctly.

waitlist_login_detect.title: Waitlist detection on login
waitlist_login_detect.body: If a waitlisted user tries to log in, they now receive a clear status message instead of a generic error.

direct_approve_flow.title: Approval creates account instantly
direct_approve_flow.body: Approving a lead now creates the account immediately. The user receives an email to log in with their credentials, no magic link required.

telegram_waitlist_alert.title: Telegram lead alerts
telegram_waitlist_alert.body: The admin now receives a real-time Telegram notification whenever a new user joins the waitlist.

telegram_approve_command.title: Fast Telegram approval
telegram_approve_command.body: Admins can now approve waitlist leads with a single click directly from the Telegram alert message.

waitlist_pills.title: Suggestion Buttons
waitlist_pills.body: Added quick suggestion buttons to fill out motivation on waitlist registration.

silent_approval.title: Silent approval
silent_approval.body: Admins can create accounts without emails.

landing_showcase.title: Visual Feature Showcase
landing_showcase.body: The landing page now displays real screenshots of every feature: Verdict, Bayesian Engine, Alerts, Inspector, and Metrics. Each block includes a language-specific image.

beta_reactivation_email_trigger.title: Beta Reactivation Trigger
beta_reactivation_email_trigger.body: You can now safely trigger the beta reactivation email from the Alert Piano inside the admin dashboard.

personalized_email_sender.title: Personalized Email Sender
personalized_email_sender.body: The email sender name now displays as 'Ivan from IronRisk' or 'Iván de IronRisk' to humanize the contact format.

fix_ci_pipeline.title: Fix CI Pipeline
fix_ci_pipeline.body: Fixed missing pytest in Github Actions.

fix_email_unicode_header.title: Email Header Unicode Fix
fix_email_unicode_header.body: Patched SMTP strict-layer rejection formatting for accented sender names.

add_regression_test_email.title: Email Regression
add_regression_test_email.body: Added a regression test to ensure emails are correctly sent using formataddr.

minimalist_email_template.title: Minimalist Beta Email
minimalist_email_template.body: Stripped heavy CSS and layouts from the beta reactivation email to hit Primary inboxes by mimicking organic interpersonal format.

add_broadcast_script.title: Automated Broadcast Script
add_broadcast_script.body: Added CI/CD architecture to trigger mass mailing through secure GitHub Actions environment variables.
